one_time_description: Roseau River 1903 Trust annual trust payment of $5,550,025 including $3,500,000 in additional loan proceeds transferred from the 1903 Trust refinancing, significantly higher than prior year $1,296,567
economic_notes: First Nation operates gaming (Niichi Oasis Gaming Centre via Roseau River Anishinabe Limited Partnership), construction (The Creek Construction LP and Inc.), and several numbered Manitoba Ltd. companies as government business enterprises. GBEs had combined revenue of $10,224,509 and net loss of $205,552 in 2022. Community Development Account funded by annual trust payments from Roseau River 1903 Trust which holds an RBC loan of $13,100,000 as of March 31, 2022.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2022 — audit report signed April 8, 2024, nearly two years after year end, which is highly unusual. Nine entities consolidated line-by-line and nine GBEs accounted for by modified equity method. The 1903 Trust is not consolidated but makes annual trust payments to the First Nation's Community Development Account; the Trust holds a $13.1M RBC loan. Tobacco tax rebate from Province of Manitoba is a significant own-source revenue stream. No schedule of remuneration provided in the extracted text. Construction in progress of $388,094 for a 3-unit housing project shown separately on balance sheet. Due to Roseau River Anishinabe First Nation Trust of $1,221,407 represents a long-term obligation from a prior guarantee arrangement.
